channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Data center vacancies hit historic lows despite record construction
item.description: Space supply in major data center markets increased by 34% year-over-year to 6,922.6 megawatts, considerable growth over the 26% increase in 2023 , according to CBRE research.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Job gains in Silicon Valley grind to halt
item.description: Hiring in Silicon Valley has staggered to a halt, yet per-capita income has hopped higher, a new report shows.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Invincible season 3 episode 7 just made good on a two-year-old Instagram post and a wild rumor about Jeffrey Dean Morgan
item.description: Invincible season 3's penultimate episode finally reveals who's voicing Conquest in the hit Prime Video show.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Pixel Phone and Watch Updates: Camera Upgrade, Kernel Upgrades, and More
item.description: Google has released updates for its Pixel phones and watches, including an upgrade to the Pixel Camera app and kernel upgrades for older Pixel handsets.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How Trump's tariffs could pinch the tech sector
item.description: The tech industry is set to feel the pinch as the Trump administration levies new tariffs against key U.S. trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: Utah has become the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: This shaving brand was banned on TikTok, so it started an OnlyFans
item.description: How can a razor brand market its products online when the word razor is shadow banned? Start an OnlyFans, of course. London-based Nimbi, which launched at Erewhon in January 2024, began posting on TikTok last summer but noticed its views were disappointingly low.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Why “café-like branches” are the latest buzz among CIOs
item.description: In the fast-paced world of technology, it’s rare for an idea to capture the attention of CIOs and IT leaders universally—but the concept of the “café-like branch” has done just that.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Pentagon signs AI deal to aid military decision-making
item.description: The Department of Defense has signed a contract with start-up Scale AI to use artificial intelligence for military planning and operations, marking the Pentagon's latest incorporation of emerging tech into its workflows.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump calls CHIPS Act ‘horrible,’ wants to defund it
item.description: In his address to Congress last night, President Donald J. Trump called the CHIPS Act “horrible,” and said whatever money hasn’t been spent should now be used to reduce the national debt. The CHIPS Act, enacted into law in 2022 under then-President Joseph R. Biden Jr.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Judge denies Musk's move against OpenAI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump divides crypto world with plans for strategic reserve
item.description: President Trump’s plan for a strategic crypto reserve has stirred mixed feelings within the cryptocurrency industry. The digital assets space has largely welcomed Trump’s mission to boost the industry, which faced heavy regulatory scrutiny under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: This DOGE staffer’s GitHub posts might help us understand how Elon Musk wants to bring AI into the government
item.description: The internet posts and side projects of Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) worker Jordan Wick could give some clues for how Musk’s efficiency group might attempt to use AI to downsize and retool the government.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: A federal judge has denied Elon Musk’s request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VMware ESXi gets critical patches for in-the-wild virtual machine escape attack
item.description: Broadcom released emergency patches for its VMware ESXi, Workstation, and Fusion products to fix three vulnerabilities that can lead to virtual machine escape and are actively being exploited by attackers.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Casey Anthony, mom acquitted in toddler's death, joins TikTok
item.description: Casey Anthony, a Florida mother who was acquitted of murder charges in the death of her 2-year-old daughter Caylee, has joined TikTok to brand herself as a "legal advocate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: TypeScript 5.8 reaches general availability
item.description: Microsoft’s TypeScript 5.8, the latest version of the company’s strongly typed superset of JavaScript, has reached general availability (GA).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas
item.description: Federal use cases typically fall into one of five “swim lanes,” with most impacting customer or citizen services, said the IDC Insight's federal researcher. The post AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
